questions.q: In Hindu tradition, which deity does the mighty eagle Garuda serve as the vahana (mount)?
questions.explanation: Garuda is celebrated in the Puranas as the eagle vahana of Vishnu, bearing him across the cosmos.

questions.q: What kind of creature is Garuda usually depicted as in Hindu iconography?
questions.explanation: Garuda is depicted as a mighty eagle-king, often shown with a bird's wings and beak combined with a human-like form.

questions.q: Garuda is famously hailed as the king of which group of animals?
questions.explanation: Garuda is honored in the Puranas as the king of birds (Pakshiraja).

questions.q: Which creatures are Garuda's eternal enemies, whom he is known as a devourer of?
questions.explanation: Garuda bears an enduring enmity with the serpent Nagas and is hailed as a devourer of snakes.

questions.q: Who is the mother of Garuda in the Puranic accounts?
questions.explanation: Garuda is the son of Vinata, whose enslavement to the Nagas he sought to undo.

questions.q: Which great sage is named as the father of Garuda?
questions.explanation: Garuda is the son of the sage Kashyapa and his wife Vinata, according to the Puranas.

questions.q: To free his enslaved mother from the Nagas, what precious substance did Garuda set out to win?
questions.explanation: The Puranas relate that Garuda won the amrita, the nectar of immortality, to free his mother Vinata from the Nagas.

questions.q: Which Hindu deity, whose mount is Garuda, is known as the preserver of the cosmos?
questions.explanation: Garuda serves Vishnu, the preserver of the Trimurti, as his eagle vahana.

questions.q: Garuda's enmity arose because his mother Vinata had been enslaved by the serpents, who were the children of which figure?
questions.explanation: The Nagas were the offspring of Kadru, whose sons held Vinata, Garuda's mother, in bondage.

questions.q: Garuda serves as the vahana of Vishnu. In Hindu tradition, what is a vahana?
questions.explanation: A vahana is the divine animal mount or vehicle of a deity, and Garuda is the vahana of Vishnu.

questions.q: In the Puranic account, who is named as the mother of the serpent Nagas, the rival of Garuda's mother Vinata?
questions.explanation: Kadru, another wife of Kashyapa, was the mother of the Nagas, and her sons enslaved Vinata.

questions.q: Garuda and Aruna, the charioteer of Surya, are described in the Puranas as sons of the same mother. Who is she?
questions.explanation: Both Garuda and Aruna, the dawn-charioteer of Surya, are sons of Vinata in the Puranic genealogy.

questions.q: Which king of the devas opposed Garuda when he flew to seize the nectar of immortality?
questions.explanation: Indra, guardian of the amrita, confronted Garuda with his thunderbolt during the quest for the nectar.

questions.q: Although Garuda won the nectar of immortality for the Nagas as ransom, the serpents never actually drank it. Why, in the Puranas?
options: It was hidden in the ocean
Indra snatched the pot away before they returned from bathing
Vishnu took it back before they could drink
questions.explanation: While the Nagas went to purify themselves before drinking, Indra reclaimed the amrita, so the serpents never tasted it.

questions.q: On the great Garuda figure that adorns Vishnu temples, in what posture is Garuda most often shown before the Lord?
questions.explanation: Garuda is typically depicted kneeling or standing with hands folded in devotion (anjali) before Vishnu, as his foremost servant.

questions.q: The tall pillar or column placed before the sanctum in many Vishnu temples, surmounted by his eagle mount, is known by what name?
questions.explanation: The Garuda Stambha, a pillar bearing the image of Garuda, traditionally stands before the sanctum of Vishnu temples.

questions.q: Which Mahapurana is named after Vishnu's eagle mount and is traditionally framed as Vishnu's discourse to Garuda?
questions.explanation: The Garuda Purana is one of the eighteen Mahapuranas, presented as Vishnu's teaching to Garuda and known especially for its accounts of the afterlife.

questions.q: Both Garuda and the Nagas are counted among the offspring of which common father, by his different wives?
questions.explanation: The sage Kashyapa is father of both Garuda (through Vinata) and the Nagas (through Kadru), making them half-siblings.

questions.q: In the Mahabharata, before Garuda became the mount of Vishnu, the Lord granted him a boon and received a request in return. What did Garuda ask?
options: To be immortal and free of disease, even without drinking the amrita
questions.explanation: The Mahabharata relates that Vishnu granted Garuda immortality and freedom from decay without his needing to drink the nectar.

questions.q: Garuda is the vahana of Vishnu. Which animal serves as the corresponding vahana of Shiva?
questions.explanation: While Garuda is the mount of Vishnu, the bull Nandi is the vahana of Shiva.

questions.q: In Hindu iconography, what does Garuda most commonly carry upon his back or shoulders?
questions.explanation: Garuda is most often shown bearing Vishnu, frequently with his consort Lakshmi, across the heavens.

questions.q: In the Ramayana, which noble vulture, counted in tradition among Garuda's kindred, fought Ravana to stop the abduction of Sita?
questions.explanation: Jatayu is the vulture who confronted Ravana as he carried off Sita; in tradition he and his brother Sampati are reckoned among the kindred of Garuda's line.

questions.q: When Garuda flew with the pot of nectar, he is said to have carried it without drinking, showing his selflessness. Who, pleased by this restraint, offered him a boon making him their mount?
questions.explanation: Vishnu, pleased that Garuda bore the amrita without drinking it for himself, made him his eternal vahana.

questions.q: According to the Mahabharata's account, what did Garuda do that led the Nagas to release his mother Vinata from bondage?
options: He slew all the serpents in battle
He brought them the amrita as the agreed ransom
questions.explanation: The Nagas had demanded the amrita as the price of Vinata's freedom, and Garuda fetched it to fulfill the ransom.

questions.q: In the Bhagavata Purana, when the elephant-king Gajendra cried out to be saved from the crocodile, how did Vishnu arrive at the lake?
questions.explanation: The Gajendra Moksha episode describes Vishnu rushing to the elephant's aid mounted upon Garuda, his swift eagle vahana.

questions.q: Garuda is sometimes given the epithet 'Suparna.' What does this name signify?
questions.explanation: Suparna, an early Vedic name applied to Garuda, means 'having beautiful or fair wings.'

questions.q: In the Mahabharata, Garuda's vast hunger once led him toward a giant tortoise and a giant elephant locked in combat. What were these two beings, according to the tale?
options: Two sages reborn as animals through a curse from their feud
questions.explanation: The Mahabharata relates that the tortoise and elephant Garuda seized were two brothers, sages cursed into those forms after a bitter quarrel.

questions.q: Garuda is sometimes invoked as 'Vishnu-ratha.' What does this title mean?
questions.explanation: 'Vishnu-ratha' means 'the chariot (vehicle) of Vishnu,' reflecting Garuda's role as the Lord's bearer.

questions.q: Which serpent of Hindu lore, on whom Vishnu reclines upon the cosmic ocean, belongs to the Naga race that Garuda traditionally opposes?
questions.explanation: Ananta Shesha is the great serpent of the Naga race, the lineage with which Garuda holds his enduring enmity, though both serve Vishnu.

questions.q: Garuda's image is so identified with Vishnu's protection that, in tradition, his form and mantra are especially invoked against what?
questions.explanation: As the foremost foe of serpents, Garuda is traditionally invoked for protection against snakebite and poison.
